# Tokio Capability Risk Register

**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.1.1.3` ([T1.1.c])
**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
**Date**: 2026-03-03
**Parent inventory**: `docs/tokio_ecosystem_capability_inventory.md` (T1.1.a)
**Parent evidence map**: `docs/tokio_capability_evidence_map.md` (T1.1.b)
**Purpose**: Assign objective maturity and risk levels per capability family.
This document is the baseline risk register for TOKIO-REPLACE execution planning.

---

## 1. Risk Assessment Methodology

### 1.1 Maturity Level (M)

| Level | Code | Criteria |
|-------|------|----------|
| Mature | M4 | Stable API, audited, 200+ tests, no open bugs |
| Active | M3 | Functional API, 50+ tests, minor gaps |
| Early | M2 | Core path works, API unstable, <50 tests or known gaps |
| Parked | M1 | Scaffolding only or work paused |
| Planned | M0 | No code, tracked in beads only |

### 1.2 Replacement Risk (R)

| Level | Code | Criteria |
|-------|------|----------|
| Low | R1 | Complete parity, well-tested, low interop friction |
| Medium | R2 | Active parity with known minor gaps, moderate test coverage |
| High | R3 | Partial parity, significant gaps, or high interop friction |
| Critical | R4 | Blocking gaps for replacement claim, or no implementation |

### 1.3 Determinism Score (D)

| Level | Code | Criteria |
|-------|------|----------|
| Full | D3 | Fully deterministic in LabRuntime |
| Partial | D2 | Core logic deterministic, I/O paths real-time |
| None | D1 | Not wired into lab infrastructure |

### 1.4 Composite Risk Priority

```
Priority = R Г— (5 - M) Г— (4 - D)
```

Higher values indicate higher urgency for remediation.

---

## 2. Risk Register

| ID | Family | Maturity | Risk | Determ. | Tests | Priority | Blockers / Key Risks |
|----|--------|----------|------|---------|-------|----------|---------------------|
| F01 | Core Runtime | M4 | R1 | D3 | 1,100 | 1 | None. Fully proven, heavily tested. |
| F02 | Structured Concurrency | M4 | R1 | D3 | 1,070 | 1 | None. 6/6 invariants Lean-proven. Asupersync advantage. |
| F03 | Channels | M4 | R1 | D3 | 345 | 1 | None. Two-phase reserve/commit exceeds Tokio. |
| F04 | Sync Primitives | M4 | R1 | D3 | 295 | 1 | None. All six Tokio primitives covered + extras. |
| F05 | Time / Timers | M4 | R1 | D3 | 234 | 1 | None. Virtual time wheel for determinism. |
| F06 | Async I/O | M3 | R2 | D2 | 198 | 4 | Missing: `tokio-util::io::SinkWriter` adapter. `ReaderStream`/`StreamReader` are implemented in `src/io/stream_adapters.rs`. |
| F07 | Codec / Framing | M3 | R2 | D2 | 77 | 4 | Low test coverage. Need: conformance tests for production codecs. |
| F08 | Byte Buffers | M4 | R1 | D3 | 86 | 1 | None. Complete Bytes/BytesMut implementation. |
| F09 | Reactor | M3 | R2 | D2 | 247 | 4 | T2.6 parity contract added for epoll/kqueue/IOCP/io_uring readiness+reregister+wake-dedup semantics; continue io_uring performance hardening. |
| F10 | TCP/UDP/Unix | M3 | R2 | D2 | 677 | 4 | Well-tested but mixed determinism for real I/O. |
| F11 | DNS | M3 | R2 | D2 | 44 | 4 | Low test count. Needs: caching, TTL, resolver conformance. |
| F12 | TLS | M3 | R2 | D2 | 134 | 4 | rustls-only (no native-tls). Feature-gated, well-tested. |
| F13 | WebSocket | M3 | R2 | D2 | 185 | 4 | Native impl (no tungstenite). Good test coverage. |
| F14 | HTTP/1+2 | M3 | R2 | D2 | 717 | 4 | Native impl. Pre-existing compile error in h2/connection.rs (in-flight fix by another agent). |
| F15 | QUIC + HTTP/3 | M1 | **R4** | D1 | 472 | **48** | **CRITICAL.** T4.2/T4.3 transport parity is closed (handshake lifecycle, stream transitions, loss-recovery, flow/congestion stress correctness) and native `quic`/`http3` surfaces are exposed. T4.4 hardens key-update lifecycle plus native 0-RTT/resumption and migration guards in `quic_native` state machines. Remaining blocker is broad interop matrix closure. Legacy quinn/h3 wrappers remain compat-only and off by default due Tokio-transitive risk. |
| F16 | Web Framework | M3 | R2 | D2 | 111 | 4 | Missing: multipart, SSE, cookie extraction, CORS. Moderate gap. |
| F17 | gRPC | M3 | R2 | D2 | 235 | 4 | Native impl (no tonic). Good coverage for unary/streaming. |
| F18 | Database | M3 | R2 | D2 | 168 | 4 | Feature-gated. Missing: connection pool abstraction. Wire protocols present. |
| F19 | Messaging | M2 | **R3** | D2 | 185 | **12** | Missing: AMQP, MQTT. Redis/NATS/Kafka present but early maturity. |
| F20 | Service/Middleware | M3 | R2 | D3 | 193 | 2 | Tower adapter available. Native service trait cancel-aware. |
| F21 | Filesystem | M2 | **R3** | D2 | 107 | **12** | Early maturity. Missing: some convenience ops (symlink, copy, canonicalize). VFS incomplete. |
| F22 | Process | M3 | R2 | D2 | 17 | 4 | Low test count (17). Needs lifecycle and cancellation testing. |
| F23 | Signals | M3 | R2 | D2 | 43 | 4 | Reasonable coverage. Platform-specific (non-wasm). |
| F24 | Streams | M3 | R1 | D3 | 198 | 2 | Comprehensive adapter set. Lab-compatible. |
| F25 | Observability | M3 | R2 | D2 | 262 | 4 | Native + optional OpenTelemetry. Spectral health is unique. |
| F26 | Lab/Testing | M4 | R1 | D3 | 858 | 1 | None. Asupersync differentiator. No Tokio equivalent. |
| F27 | Combinators | M3 | R1 | D3 | 580 | 2 | Well beyond Tokio's tower middleware. Algebraic laws proven. |
| F28 | Interop | M0 | **R4** | D1 | 10 | **60** | **CRITICAL.** No dedicated compatibility crate. Blocks claims for Tokio-locked crate migration (reqwest, sqlx, sea-orm, etc.). |

## 4. Critical Path Analysis

### 4.1 Replacement-Blocking Items (R4)

| Priority | Family | Action Required | Track |
|----------|--------|-----------------|-------|
| 60 | F28 вҖ” Interop | Design + implement compatibility crate for Tokio-locked ecosystems | T7 |
| 48 | F15 вҖ” QUIC/H3 | Complete native QUIC transport, expose feature flags, finish H3 | T4 |

### 4.2 High-Risk Items (R3)

| Priority | Family | Action Required | Track |
|----------|--------|-----------------|-------|
| 12 | F19 вҖ” Messaging | Add AMQP + MQTT clients; harden Redis/NATS/Kafka | T6 |
| 12 | F21 вҖ” Filesystem | Complete API surface; finish VFS for lab determinism | T3 |

### 4.3 Remediation Waves (Recommended Execution Order)

**Wave 1 вҖ” Critical blockers** (must resolve for any replacement claim):
1. F28: Design interop architecture (T7.1-T7.2)
2. F15: Unpark QUIC, complete transport core (T4.1-T4.2)

**Wave 2 вҖ” High-risk closure** (needed for credible replacement):
3. F21: Filesystem API gaps (T3.2-T3.3)
4. F19: AMQP + MQTT clients (T6, new sub-tasks)

**Wave 3 вҖ” Medium-risk hardening** (strengthen active surfaces):
5. F07: Codec conformance tests
6. F11: DNS resolver hardening
7. F22: Process lifecycle tests
8. F16: Web framework middleware gaps
9. F18: Database connection pooling

**Wave 4 вҖ” Polish and evidence** (finalize replacement evidence):
10. F06: tokio-util I/O adapter parity
11. All: Determinism coverage expansion for lab testing
12. All: Migration cookbook per domain

---

## 5. Aggregate Metrics

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total families | 28 |
| Low risk (R1) | 9 (32%) |
| Medium risk (R2) | 15 (54%) |
| High risk (R3) | 2 (7%) |
| Critical risk (R4) | 2 (7%) |
| Total test coverage | ~8,000+ tests |
| Families with formal proofs | 1 (F02 вҖ” 170 Lean theorems) |
| Full determinism | 10 families (36%) |
| Partial determinism | 17 families (61%) |
| No determinism | 1 family (F28 вҖ” planned) |

### Risk Trend Baseline

This register establishes the t=0 baseline. Subsequent passes should show:
- R4 count decreasing (blockers being resolved)
- R3 вҶ’ R2 transitions (high risk being mitigated)
- Test counts increasing for low-coverage families
- Determinism scores improving as lab wiring extends
